---
title: "SD-WAN Modernization Spec"
subtitle: "Software-Defined WAN governance specification"
doc-type: modernization-spec
audience: [Customer, Architect, Assessor]
classification: Controlled
boundary: GCC-Moderate
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml"
canon-reference:
  - "docs/customer-documents/architecture-series/six-plane-architecture.qmd"
  - "docs/docs/01_UnifiedArchitecture.qmd"
derived-from: "uiao canon"
---

# SD-WAN Modernization Spec

## Scope

Cross-adapter specification for Software-Defined WAN (SD-WAN)
modernization under the UIAO substrate. SD-WAN replaces static MPLS
WAN with policy-driven, telemetry-informed routing across diverse
transports. This spec defines the substrate contract for SD-WAN
adapters: identity-aware routing, telemetry-as-control, and canonical
claim emission for path and policy decisions.

[IMAGE-01: SD-WAN modernization domain diagram. Center shows three transport lanes (MPLS, Internet, LTE/5G) feeding a central SD-WAN orchestrator. Above the orchestrator, an arrow incoming from the Identity plane labeled "identity-aware policy". Below the orchestrator, an arrow outgoing to the Telemetry plane labeled "path/policy telemetry as canonical claims". On the right, a "Drift findings" lane showing canonical claim emissions for routing decisions, SLA breaches, and policy mismatches. Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C) on white background. No photographs, purely diagrammatic.]

## Substrate contract

SD-WAN adapters span the Network, Addressing, and Telemetry planes.
They inherit:

- **Identity-aware policy** — routing policy consumes identity
  context where applicable; SD-WAN does not maintain parallel
  identity authority.
- **Telemetry as control** — path quality, SLA telemetry, and policy
  decisions emit as canonical claims; the substrate consumes them as
  control input, not as after-the-fact reports.
- **Canonical decision records** — every policy decision (path
  selection, transport failover, application steering) is a claim
  with the full provenance envelope.

## Adapter shape

SD-WAN adapters declare:

- `mission-class`: `enforcement` (path-level enforcement),
  `telemetry` (path-quality observer), or `integration`
  (controller integration).
- `class`: `modernization` (replacing legacy WAN) or `conformance`
  (observing SD-WAN policy/state).
- `gcc-boundary` per adapter — most SD-WAN controllers operate
  outside FedRAMP-Moderate boundaries unless explicitly certified.

## Integration shape

```yaml
provenance:
  ...
sdwan_decision:
  controller_id: "{sd-wan_controller}"
  decision_kind: "path-selection | failover | application-steering | qos"
  source_site: "{site_id}"
  destination: "{site_id | application_id}"
  selected_path: "{transport_id}"
  identity_context: "{issuer_identity | n/a}"
  sla_state: "{sla_class}"
  policy_refs:
    - "{canon_policy_id}"
  evaluation_timestamp: ISO8601
```

## Control mapping

Maps onto:

- **NIST 800-53** SC (System & Communications Protection), AU
  families.
- **FedRAMP CR26** boundary and configuration controls.
- **NIST 800-207** to the extent SD-WAN supports Zero Trust posture.

## Drift considerations

- **`DRIFT-SCHEMA`** — controller policy schema diverges from
  canonical shape.
- **`DRIFT-PROVENANCE`** — telemetry chain breakage between site,
  controller, and substrate (common at boundary crossings).
- **`DRIFT-AUTHZ`** — policy decision emitted outside the declared
  scope (e.g. routing across an unauthorized transport class).
- **`DRIFT-SEMANTIC`** — SLA classification mismatch between
  controller and canonical SLA model.

## Boundary treatment

SD-WAN controllers frequently sit *outside* the GCC-Moderate
boundary. The adapter must declare its boundary disposition explicitly
(in-boundary, named exception, or compensating-controller). Controllers
that do not fit any of these categories require an authorizing ADR
before registration.

## Honest limits

- SD-WAN vendor maturity in federal cloud boundaries varies. Adapter
  authors may have to carry significant compensating-controller logic
  for vendors lacking in-boundary controllers.
- Path-quality telemetry from some SD-WAN vendors is proprietary;
  canonical claim emission may require synthesis with explicit
  `source_classification: synthesized` per claim.
- Identity-aware SD-WAN policy is not universally supported by all
  vendors; programs adopting an identity-naive SD-WAN cannot realize
  the full spec.

## Validation pairing

Pairs with the
[SD-WAN Validation Suite](../../validation-suites/domains/sdwan/sdwan.qmd).

## Related documents

- [Modernization Specs Index](../index.qmd)
- [Architecture Series — Six-Plane Architecture](../../architecture-series/six-plane-architecture.qmd)
- [Unified Architecture (canon)](../../../docs/01_UnifiedArchitecture.qmd)
- [Modernization Registry (canon)](../../../../src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml)
